text: Refactoring is an essential part of software development. It means improving the structure of existing code without changing its external behavior. The goal is to make the code cleaner, more readable, and easier to maintain in the future. During refactoring, developers restructure code, rename variables, or break large functions into smaller, logical pieces. However, they do not add new features or modify how the program works. One major reason to refactor is to reduce technical debt, which accumulates when developers write quick, messy code to meet deadlines. Addressing this debt regularly keeps the codebase healthy, saves time later, and helps teams work more efficiently.
